extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon defended 40-second GC lead in 10.1km San Benedetto ITT, winning Tirreno by 17 seconds over MICKA, becoming first British winner.
tactical_patterns: GANNA Filippo shattered Cancellara's 2016 course record (11:08) by 26 seconds at 10:42, resetting ITT power benchmarks.
THOMAS Geraint posted 4th fastest time at +28s to Ganna, signaling return to peak form despite not contending GC.
MICKA Rafal rode defensively to protect 2nd place rather than maximize speed, finishing 40s slower than expected.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Destroyed 4-year-old Cancellara course record by 26 seconds, establishing new ITT dominance hierarchy for short efforts
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 4th fastest time only 28s behind Ganna suggests return to 2018 Tour-winning form despite not racing for GC
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Defended 40s lead with sub-11-minute ITT at 50kph average, showing improved TT capacity for pure climber
rider_signals.MICKA Rafal: neutral: Conservative ride to secure 2nd overall, barely held off Thomas despite starting with 23-second buffer
